title: Scenario
description: Contract for a testkit catalog scenario (expected-result.json): the workflow to drive, the ordered human gate responses, and the assertions to project onto the captured run. The runtime loaders parse scenarios leniently; this schema is the test-only validation gate.

^_.description: Underscore-prefixed keys (e.g. _note) are author comments; ignored by the lenient loader.

type.description: The human-gate verb. input/review/stepApproval/escalation resume the standard pauses; toolApprove/toolReject resolve an AWAITING_TOOL_APPROVAL pause; toolContinue/toolRetry resolve an AWAITING_TOOL_DECISION pause.

reason.description: Rejection reason recorded for a toolReject gate; ignored by the other gate types, which use note.

toolInvocationId.description: Optional explicit pending tool-invocation id for a tool gate; when omitted the harness auto-targets the run's single current pending invocation (failing closed when there is not exactly one).

failedBecause.description: Fragment the recorded failure reason must contain (implies status FAILED). Pins the specific rejection a failure scenario provokes rather than merely that the run ended FAILED.

contextPresent.description: Context keys that must be present, asserted by presence only (not value). Use for fields whose magnitude is not fully deterministic, e.g. sized token estimates.

contextMatches.description: Context key -> regular expression the value must match. Use for shape assertions (e.g. an enum grade or a numeric range) rather than brittle exact equality.

notEmittedEvents.description: Event type names that must not have been emitted. Use to prove a path was genuinely not taken (e.g. no completion event on a failure route).

absentFiles.description: File paths that must not have been created. Use to prove side effects did not happen (e.g. no files written before an approval gate).

stepVisitCounts.description: Step id -> the exact number of times it must have been visited. Use for repeat-count assertions (e.g. SPAR rounds, retry attempts) rather than simple visited/not-visited presence.

orderedSteps.description: A subsequence of step ids that must occur in this relative order among all visited steps. Use to assert relative ordering rather than visitedSteps' unordered presence check.
